title: gouldia glabra var. parvithyrsa
intro: Gouldia glabra var. parvithyrsa, also known as the Small-leaved Gouldia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is a shrub native to Hawaii, and its leaves and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, and protect against infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Gouldia glabra var. parvithyrsa are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.
